page: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supporting hyper-threading, AVX, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instructions, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 66 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark data indicates that the D8s_v3 delivers moderate multi-core performance but exhibits limitations in single-core and memory-intensive operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and PassMark CPU physics test both score in the average tier, with values of 6563.83 and 859.11 respectively. However, single-core performance is weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 848.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1246.28. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read-only bandwidth measured at 35.34 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 21.67 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers.

The primary tradeoff of this instance lies in its older Intel Xeon architecture, which results in lower single-threaded efficiency and reduced decompression speeds, as evidenced by a poor bzip3 decompression score. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D8s_v3 offers a balanced resource density of vCPUs to memory for legacy workloads that do not require high-frequency processing or advanced vector extensions. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, moderate database operations, basic web serving, and lightweight LLM inference tasks using small models, where prompt processing and text generation achieve average-tier throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores) running at 2.3 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, 66 GB of local storage, and supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show average multi-core CPU and physics performance, but weak single-core execution and memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds at 35.34 GB/sec. Lacking AVX-512, it is less suited for heavy vector computations. Qualitatively, it offers a balanced resource profile for legacy applications. It is best utilized for general-purpose workloads, moderate web serving, and lightweight LLM inference using small models.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose VM featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 66 GB local storage. It offers average multi-core performance for standard workloads but has weak single-core speeds.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose VM with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 66 GB local storage, suitable for standard workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine offering dedicated Intel Xeon cores and premium storage capability for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor
32 GB of system memory and 66 GB of local storage
Premium Storage capable for enhanced disk performance
Average multi-core CPU and physics benchmark performance
Weak single-core processing and memory bandwidth limits
Best suited for general-purpose applications and light web serving
